title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. saharae
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. saharae, commonly known as the Saharan kidney vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. saharae include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, which can contribute to several health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of cancer, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its flowers and leaves, which can be consumed as teas, infusions, or added to topical creams and ointments for its medicinal effects.
